lessonTitle: Curative Therapies — Bone Marrow/Stem Cell Transplant and Gene Therapy

items.html: Identify curative options for patients with SCD
Review steps for referring patients to a transplant team
Identify the different conditioning regimens for transplant
Discuss gene therapy and how it works
Contrast the two approved gene therapy options

data.bubble_text: I am excited to share with you the most promising therapies for Sickle Cell Disease — curative options!

slides.title: Bone Marrow Transplant (BMT) / Hematopoietic Stem Cell Transplant (HSCT)

items.html: BMT/HSCT is the only proven curative therapy for SCD
Allogeneic BMT (from a donor) is the most common type
First BMT for SCD was performed in 1982

Patients who had the best 5-year event-free survival rate were: children under 12 years of age with a matched related sibling donor who did not have severe SCD-related organ damage prior to transplant
GVHD (Graft Versus Host Disease) remains a major complication of allogeneic stem cell transplant in adults
In children with a matched sibling donor, the overall survival is 95%, event-free survival is 91%
Only 15-20% of individuals with SCD have a matched sibling donor
Results with haploidentical (parent or sibling) and matched unrelated donors (MUDs) are improving
Availability and access remain major barriers to BMT/HSCT

data.html:
Quality of life impairment due to sickle cell disease complications is an indication to refer for consideration of a curative therapy.

Photo credit: Brazauskas et al., 2020

items.html: Stroke
Recurrent Acute Chest Syndrome
Recurrent severe pain
Transcranial Doppler (TCD) velocity > 200 cm/s
Bilateral proliferative retinopathy
Renal insufficiency
Sickle hepatopathy
Alloimmunization on chronic transfusion therapy
At least 3 hospitalizations/year despite HU

data.html:
Photo credit: bethematch.org, 2024

HLA Identical Sibling Donor

  • Gold standard for BMT in SCD
  • Lower risk of GVHD and graft failure
  • Best outcomes in children < 12 years old

Haploidentical (Haplo) Donor

  • Parent or sibling with 50% HLA match
  • Higher risk of GVHD and graft failure
  • Post-transplant cyclophosphamide (PT-Cy) reduces risk of GVHD
  • Expanding availability as Haplo BMT protocols improve
Let us review the outcomes data for BMT in SCD.

items.html: 1,000 patients with SCD who underwent HLA-identical sibling BMT
Overall survival 92.9%
Event-free survival 91.4%
Risk of acute GVHD: 14.8%
Risk of chronic GVHD: 14.9%
Children under 12 years old have best outcomes
Matched sibling donor superior to haploidentical or MUD
GVHD risk increases with mismatched donors

rows.myeloablative: Aims to destroy (cancer and) stem cells
rows.reduced_intensity: Aims to reduce stem cells to make space for new ones
More chimerism and increased risk of graft rejection

slides.title: Typical Process of Gene Therapy for Hemoglobinopathies

data.html: Gene therapy for hemoglobinopathies involves the following steps:

items.html: Erythrocytapheresis for >2 months prior to mobilization
Plerixafor mobilization (no G-CSF in SCD)
Targeting >2 × 10⁶ CD34+ cells/kg
Busulfan full myeloablation
Transfection/electroporation and expansion
Approximately 10 weeks from mobilization to transplant

Lentiviral Gene Therapy (Gene Addition) — Adds a functional copy of the β-globin gene (or anti-sickling variant); Semi-random integration into the genome; Self-inactivating lentiviral vector; Basis of lovo-cel (betibeglogene spartacel / Lyfgenia)
CRISPR-Cas9 Gene Editing (Gene Editing) — Edits the BCL11A enhancer to reactivate HbF production; Targeted edit — not random integration; Basis of exa-cel (exagamglogene autotemcel / Casgevy)

data.html: Key properties of Lentiviral Vectors (LVV) for gene therapy:

items.html: Replication defective
Self-inactivating (reduces insertional oncogenesis)
Vector packaging capacity (10 kb)
Semi-random integration
Variations in vector copy number (∝ transduction efficiency)

data.html: Key mechanism of CRISPR-Cas9 gene editing for SCD:

items.html: BCL11A suppresses γ-globin production, which makes HbF
γ-globin production can be reactivated by inactivating BCL11A in erythroid precursors
CRISPR-Cas9 cuts BCL11A enhancer in CD34+ stem cells → permanent HbF reactivation

data.html:
Photo credit: Frangoul, 2021

Clinical data (Frangoul, 2021):

  • Patient 1 (transfusion-dependent β-thalassemia, Panel A): dramatic increase in HbF and total Hb post-treatment
  • Patient 2 (SCD, Panel D): HbF increase to >40% of total Hb; no VOCs or hospitalizations after treatment

items.html: Betibeglogene Spartacel (lovo-cel / Lyfgenia™) — FDA approved December 8, 2023; Lentiviral vector (LVV) approach; Adds anti-sickling βA-T87Q-globin gene; For patients 12 years and older with severe SCD

Exagamglogene Autotemcel (exa-cel / Casgevy™) — FDA approved December 8, 2023; Health Canada approved; CRISPR-Cas9 gene editing approach; Reactivates HbF via BCL11A editing; For patients 12 years and older with severe SCD (recurrent VOCs)

slides.title: Steps for Sending a Gene Therapy Letter

data.html: Select, in order, the steps for sending a gene therapy letter to your transplant centre:

items.html: Identify eligible patient
Complete the letter
Send the letter to the gene therapy/BMT centre

slides.title: Exagamglogene Autotemcel (exa-cel) for Severe Sickle Cell Disease

rows.detail: Global, multicenter, open-label, single-arm, 2-year Phase 3 trial of a single infusion of exa-cel (NCT03745287)
Proportion of participants free of severe VOCs for ≥12 consecutive months (VF12)
Proportion of participants free from in-patient hospitalization for severe VOCs for ≥12 consecutive months (HF12)

slides.title: Exagamglogene Autotemcel for Severe Sickle Cell Disease

items.html: 44 participants, 12 to 35 years of age with SCD and ≥2 severe VOCs per year in the previous 2 years
30 participants followed for ≥16 months after exa-cel infusion were analysed
96.7% (29/30) had no severe VOCs for ≥12 consecutive months (VF12)
Mean VOC-free duration: 22.4 months
100% of participants free from in-patient hospitalization for severe VOCs for ≥12 consecutive months
Safety profile consistent with myeloablative Busulfan conditioning and autologous HSCT
No malignancies and no safety adverse events related to exa-cel

items.title: Lactate Dehydrogenase (LDH)
items.html: Mean levels normalized by Month 9

items.title: Haptoglobin
items.html: All patients had increases in haptoglobin to detectable levels after month 6, which is consistent with suppression of haemolysis

items.title: Absolute reticulocyte count
items.html: Mean improvement from baseline

items.title: Sustainability
items.html: All the improvements were durable

data.html: RUBY (NCT04853576) and EdiThal (NCT05444894) are Phase I/II, multi-center, open-label, single-arm studies evaluating the safety, efficacy, and tolerability of reni-cel in patients with severe SCD and transfusion-dependent thalassemia. Focus: SCD patients in RUBY trial.

items.html: No Vaso-Occlusive events (VOEs) have been observed in patients with SCD since infusion with reni-cel
Compared with a mean (SD) of 4.0 (1.4) severe VOEs/year in the 2 years before enrollment (n=10), all patients are completely VOE-free post-reni-cel infusion
After receiving the last RBC transfusion at 0.5–2.2 months post reni-cel infusion, all 5 patients have been transfusion-free for a range of 1.8–7.5 months

slides.title: Lived Experience Advocate: How Has Your Journey Changed?

data.bubble_text: Emmanuel is a Lived Experience Advocate who felt it was important to share where things are for him post transplant and what his experience was like. Let's watch a video from him now.

items.html: Bone marrow transplant and gene therapy are the two curative options for individuals with Sickle Cell Disease
Children under 12 years of age with a matched related sibling donor have the best 5-year event-free survival rate
Exa-cel and Reni-cel are both showing great efficacy and promise for individuals with severe VOEs
